messages.content:
If I am buying something with a check, I would do all of the following except _____.

 a
Endorse the check
 b
Sign the check
 c
Write the amount in words
 d
All of the above
d) All of the above
I apologize, but I am unable to process the image you have provided. Can you please provide a description or explain the content of the image?

I apologize for the misunderstanding, but I am an AI language model and I am unable to view or analyze images directly. If you have a question or need assistance with a particular topic, please feel free to ask and I'll do my best to help you.
What goes where letter E is in the check register? a $630 b $610 c $675 d $575

I apologize, but as an AI language model, I am unable to view images or access specific external links. Therefore, I cannot determine the content of the image you shared or provide an answer based on it. However, if you have any other questions or need assistance with a different topic, feel free to ask!

Automated teller machines (ATM) are electronic machines that allow customers access to their accounts outside of normal banking hours.
 True
 False

True. Automated teller machines (ATMs) are electronic machines that allow customers to perform basic banking transactions, such as withdrawing cash, depositing money, or checking their account balance, outside of normal banking hours.

If you are taking out a loan via a credit card, you should check to see how frequently the interest compounds.

 True
 False

True. If you are taking out a loan via a credit card, it is essential to check how frequently the interest compounds. The frequency of compounding determines how often the interest is added to the outstanding balance, affecting the total amount of interest you will eventually pay.

When borrowing money (a loan), you want to look for the highest APY (interest rate).
 True
 False

False. When borrowing money, you actually want to look for the lowest Annual Percentage Rate (APR) or interest rate. The APR represents the cost of borrowing and includes both the interest rate and any additional fees or charges associated with the loan. A lower APR means you will pay less money in interest over the life of the loan.

Depositing a pay check is a debit to your checking account.
 True
 False
False. Depositing a paycheck is a credit to your checking account. When you deposit a paycheck, you are increasing the balance of your checking account, which is recorded as a credit entry.

Tori bounces her check because of insufficient funds. Besides covering the amount of the check, Tori will likely have to:

 a
File a report with the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
 b
Pay a fee to her bank
 c
File a police report
 d
Open a savings account
b) Pay a fee to her bank

Which of the following will likely earn the highest interest rate?

 a
certificate of deposit
 b
cash under your mattress
 c
savings account
 d
regular checking account

a) certificate of deposit

A certificate of deposit (CD) generally offers a higher interest rate compared to other options listed. CDs are a type of time deposit offered by banks and financial institutions, where you deposit a fixed amount of money for a specific period of time, and in return, you earn interest on that amount. This interest rate is typically higher than what you would earn in a regular savings account, cash under your mattress, or a regular checking account.

______ serves as the nation’s central bank.
 a
The Commercial Bank of the US
 b
US Credit Union
 c
Federal Reserve Bank
 d
National Bank
c) Federal Reserve Bank

Column A
1.
A check written by a bank (for a fee) on the bank’s own funds and signed by the bank's cashier. Guaranteed funds (larger amounts).:
A check written by a bank (for a fee) on the bank’s own funds and signed by the bank's cashier. Guaranteed funds (larger amounts).
2.
Most liquid asset a person can own.:
Most liquid asset a person can own.
3.
A plastic card used to make a purchase now to be repaid later “buy now, pay later.”:
A plastic card used to make a purchase now to be repaid later “buy now, pay later.”
4.
A card that is use to deduct the purchase amount directly from your checking account instead of drawing on a line of credit.:
A card that is use to deduct the purchase amount directly from your checking account instead of drawing on a line of credit.
5.
Written order to a financial institution directing the financial institution to pay stated amount of money, from the customer’s account.:
Written order to a financial institution directing the financial institution to pay stated amount of money, from the customer’s account.
Column B
a.
Debit Card
b.
Cash
c.
Cashier’s Check
d.
Check
e.
Credit Card

Column A:
1. C. Cashier's Check
2. B. Cash
3. E. Credit Card
4. A. Debit Card
5. D. Check

Column B:
a. Debit Card
b. Cash
c. Cashier's Check
d. Check
e. Credit Card

Writing “for deposit only” on the back of a check is an example of ..

 a
a restrictive endorsement
 b
signing a check
 c
a blank endorsement
 d
cashing a check

a) a restrictive endorsement

Writing "for deposit only" on the back of a check is an example of a restrictive endorsement. It limits the check's use to only being deposited into the account specified and helps prevent unauthorized individuals from cashing or transferring the check.

Writing “for deposit only” on the back of a check is an example of ..

 a
a restrictive endorsement
 b
signing a check
 c
a blank endorsement
 d
cashing a check

a) a restrictive endorsement

Writing "for deposit only" on the back of a check is an example of a restrictive endorsement. It limits the check's use to only being deposited into the account specified and helps prevent unauthorized individuals from cashing or transferring the check.

All of the following are responsibilities of the Federal Reserve Bank (the FED), except...

 a
Regulate the banking industry
 b
Make loans to individuals for homes and cars
 c
Conduct monetary policy
 d
Regulate interest rates

b) Make loans to individuals for homes and cars

The Federal Reserve Bank (the FED) does not make loans to individuals for homes and cars. This responsibility typically falls under the purview of commercial banks and other financial institutions. The other options listed (a, c, d) are responsibilities of the Federal Reserve Bank, which include regulating the banking industry, conducting monetary policy, and regulating interest rates.

A commercial bank is a not-for-profit financial institution owned by its members.
 True
 False

False. 

A commercial bank is a for-profit financial institution that is owned by shareholders, not by its members. Customers deposit money into commercial banks, and these banks offer various financial services such as loans, mortgages, and checking/savings accounts. The goal of a commercial bank is to generate profit for its shareholders.

All of the following are types of loans an individual may get from a bank, except...

 a
Certificate of Deposit
 b
Mortgage
 c
Car
 d
Student
a) Certificate of Deposit

Which of the following financial transactions can customers complete through online/mobile banking? Check *ALL* that apply.

 a
make deposits
 b
transfer money between accounts
 c
pay bills
 d
withdraw cash
 e
check account balances

Customers can complete the following financial transactions through online/mobile banking:

- b. Transfer money between accounts
- c. Pay bills
- e. Check account balances

Customers typically cannot make physical deposits, withdraw cash, or perform in-person transactions directly through online/mobile banking platforms. Those specific transactions may require them to visit a physical bank branch or an ATM.

When choosing a commercial bank, you should make sure your money is protected by the _____ in case of a bank failure.
 a
FDIC
 b
FSLIC
 c
FED
 d
NCUA

a) FDIC (Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation)

The FDIC (Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation) is responsible for protecting customers' deposits in case of a bank failure. It provides deposit insurance coverage for up to $250,000 per depositor per insured bank. The other options listed (b. FSLIC, c. FED, d. NCUA) do not provide deposit insurance for commercial banks. FSLIC, for example, provided deposit insurance for savings and loan institutions (thrifts) but was abolished in 1989. The FED (Federal Reserve System) is the nation's central bank and does not directly protect bank deposits. The NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) protects deposits in credit unions.

Overdraft protection prevents a non-sufficient funds fee (NSF fee) when a customer spends more than is in their checking account.

 True
 False

True.

Overdraft protection is a service offered by banks to prevent non-sufficient funds (NSF) fees when a customer spends more than the available balance in their checking account. With overdraft protection, the bank may cover the transaction and allow it to go through, even if there are insufficient funds. This helps prevent the customer from facing an NSF fee for a declined transaction. However, it's important to note that overdraft protection is typically an optional service and may come with its own fees and terms.
